title: Knysna braces for summer amid drought
description: KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna is bracing for the...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna is bracing for the summer season as the municipality implements contingency measures to address ongoing dry conditions across the region.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna is bracing for the summer season as the municipality implements contingency measures to address ongoing dry conditions across the region."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"As of late, water levels have been under sustained pressure, prompting Council to reinforce its drought readiness plans."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knysna Mayor Thando Matika assured residents and visitors that the town is prepared to maintain water security despite the challenges.","html":"Knysna Mayor Thando Matika assured residents and visitors that the town is prepared to maintain water security despite the challenges."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"We know many residents are concerned and the holiday season is around the corner,\" Matika said. \"I want to assure our community and our visitors that we are prepared. We are taking proactive steps to protect our water supply.\"","html":"\"We know many residents are concerned and the holiday season is around the corner,\" Matika said. \"I want to assure our community and our visitors that we are prepared. We are taking proactive steps to protect our water supply.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Matika further stated that key measures include optimising existing water sources, accelerating groundwater projects, enhancing monitoring systems, and preparing additional supply options if required.","html":"Matika further stated that key measures include optimising existing water sources, accelerating groundwater projects, enhancing monitoring systems, and preparing additional supply options if required."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The municipality has also outlined stricter enforcement of Level 3 water restrictions, with fines for misuse, and intensified public awareness campaigns.","html":"The municipality has also outlined stricter enforcement of Level 3 water restrictions, with fines for misuse, and intensified public awareness campaigns."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Authorities are actively identifying properties bypassing meters or not paying for water, while the Knysna Reverse Osmosis Plant remains on standby should it be needed."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The appeal for careful water usage extends to all communities in Greater Knysna, including Sedgefield, Karatara, Buffalo Bay, Rheenendal, the Brentons, Belvidere, and Knoetzie. Council stressed that collective effort from residents and businesses is essential to protect the shared water supply during this period of aridity.","html":"The appeal for careful water usage extends to all communities in Greater Knysna, including Sedgefield, Karatara, Buffalo Bay, Rheenendal, the Brentons, Belvidere, and Knoetzie. Council stressed that collective effort from residents and businesses is essential to protect the shared water supply during this period of aridity."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Recent critically low levels in the Knysna River forced a temporary halt at the Charlesford raw water pumpstation on Sunday 23 November."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Levels have since risen in the pond from which we pump raw water out of the river,\" explained Matika. He said the pumps were offline for roughly a day, but are now fully operational.","html":"\"Levels have since risen in the pond from which we pump raw water out of the river,\" explained Matika. He said the pumps were offline for roughly a day, but are now fully operational."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Despite these challenges, Knysna remains open to tourists. \"Tourism is vital to our local economy, and we look forward to welcoming both new and returning guests this season,\" Matika said.","html":"Despite these challenges, Knysna remains open to tourists. \"Tourism is vital to our local economy, and we look forward to welcoming both new and returning guests this season,\" Matika said."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"We simply ask everyone, residents and visitors alike, to use water responsibly. With co-operation and sensible use, we can manage this period without compromising the Knysna experience.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Currently, potable water is restricted to essential household use, with non-essential activities such as garden watering and car washing still prohibited. The municipality continues to monitor water levels daily and will provide regular updates as the situation evolves.","html":"Currently, potable water is restricted to essential household use, with non-essential activities such as garden watering and car washing still prohibited. The municipality continues to monitor water levels daily and will provide regular updates as the situation evolves."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Read previous articles:","html":"Read previous articles:"},{"type":"list","items":["Knysna water crisis deepens as river pumps shut down","Knysna faces water supply strain amid ongoing drought"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Knysna braces for summer amid drought
